text: The Adam optimizer is one of the most popular optimization algorithms in deep learning.
It maintains two moving averages called moments that help guide the weight updates.
The first moment, m, tracks the exponential moving average of gradients, similar to momentum.
The second moment, v, tracks the exponential moving average of squared gradients, providing adaptive learning rates.
The update equations combine these moments to adjust the weights.
With gradient 0.8, the moments accumulate values while the weight decreases.
With gradient 0.5, the moments update smoothly, adapting to the gradient magnitude.
With gradient 0.3, the weight continues converging toward the optimal value.
Adam's brilliance lies in combining momentum with adaptive learning rates through these dual moment estimates.
